lens: Repair realism — what ships is the POST-REPAIR map; discount strategies whose nominal walk diverges wildly from post-repair reality.

S1.notes: Through my lens the worst offender: HIGH repair load from ring fragmentation at the 1.4:1 rectangle's N/S edges means the elegant concentric spiral is NOT what ships — the boundary rebalance redraws the non-square rings. Biggest gap between nominal beauty and post-repair reality. Sole true center-outward strategy (=5) and that is its only redemption. Not fatal: CO near-rectangular, repair provably restores contiguity.

S2.notes: Highest repair fidelity — what walks is what ships, honest description. But the honest product is aspect-~10 full-width bands, maximally non-square; fails the user's #1 goal outright. No center-outward, no squares-first semantics. My lens rewards the fidelity but the deliverable itself is poor.

S3.notes: Best under my lens: the bbox-side penalty is the only mechanism that itself optimizes squares (=5), and grown regions hold contiguity by construction so the bulk of the map survives repair largely intact — divergence is concentrated and known (sealed regions + final remainder), LOW-MEDIUM. Post-repair reality stays close to the square-optimizing nominal. Weakness: west-edge seed, no center-outward; partial (not total) contiguity guarantee.

S4.notes: MEDIUM repair from mask-crossing jumps; on near-rectangular CO masking is modest. Post-repair product is compact blobs — more regular than S2's bands but not square and not center-outward. Best geography_robustness (explicit fallback candidate). Honest-ish divergence but vision-empty.

S5.notes: Cleanest post-repair fidelity (LOW repair, best pre-repair balance) — what ships ~ nominal rectangle-ish wedges. High repair-realism. But embodies NONE of the user's vision: no square-mile walk, no center-outward, no squares-first; produces few large wedges, not 'as many squares as possible.' Academic baseline; high fidelity to a deliverable the user did not ask for.

steelman_against_first_choice: S3 seeds from the west edge with zero center-outward semantics, flatly violating the user's explicit "begin in the middle" instruction. Worse for my own lens: the packet calls its "by construction" contiguity guarantee PARTIAL — early-sealed regions plus the final remainder rely on repair. On a ~1.4:1 rectangle swept in ~114-wide bands against an irregular state edge, repeated early sealing could make cumulative sealed+remainder repair materially heavier than the "LOW-MEDIUM" label, eroding exactly the square-fidelity I credit it for — the post-repair map could diverge more than its nominal description suggests, which is the precise failure mode I claim to punish in S1.
